body:
## Problem

An orchestrated action can finish with an unsatisfactory result (`no_changes`, failure, or verify failure), after which the orchestrator stops without enough recovery or visibility.

Original example:

- https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77#issuecomment-4360090765

Additional recent example from PR #77:

- The review/orchestrator selected `fix-pr` for concrete unresolved findings.
- The `fix-pr` run posted `Sepo did not produce code changes`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/77#issuecomment-4363352475
- The follow-up orchestrator run received `source_action=fix-pr` and `source_conclusion=no_changes`, then stopped because the current policy only permits `fix-pr -> review` when `fix-pr` concludes `success`.

This issue tracks the orchestration-level behavior after a child action reports an unsatisfactory result. The separate root-cause/context-delivery problem for resumed `fix-pr` runs is tracked in #84.

## Current behavior

`fix-pr` only hands back to `review` when the conclusion is `success`. If `fix-pr` returns `no_changes`, the orchestrator treats the chain as terminal even if the previous review synthesis had concrete unresolved action items.

Relevant areas:

- `.agent/src/handoff.ts` — handoff policy for `fix-pr` conclusions
- `.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts` — passes source action/conclusion back to the orchestrator
- `.agent/src/response.ts` — maps no branch edits to `no_changes`
- `.github/prompts/agent-orchestrator.md` — documents planner policy

## Desired behavior

When an orchestrated action fails or produces no changes, the orchestrator should not silently dead-end. It should choose an explicit recovery/stop path, such as:

1. dispatch a follow-up `review` once to re-evaluate whether the findings are still present, especially for `fix-pr -> no_changes`; or
2. stop visibly with a parent/PR comment that includes the failed action, conclusion, and attempted handoff context; or
3. classify the chain as blocked/failed with enough structured detail for a maintainer or future orchestrator run to continue safely.

The policy should avoid infinite loops and remain bounded by the existing round budget/dedupe markers.

## Acceptance criteria

- Orchestrated `fix-pr -> no_changes` has an explicit tested behavior: re-review once, blocked stop, or another documented recovery path.
- The visible stop/error comment includes enough context to understand what action was attempted and why it did not progress.
- The orchestrator prompt and handoff policy agree on the behavior for `fix-pr` `no_changes`, `failed`, and `verify_failed`.
- Regression coverage prevents a concrete review finding from becoming an opaque `fix-pr concluded no_changes` stop with no actionable context.
- Loop prevention remains intact through round budget and handoff dedupe markers.
title: Handle orchestrated task no_changes/failure visibly and recover when safe
